data.content:
### Auto-Close Duplicates TypeScript Implementation

Source: https://context7.com/anthropics/claude-code/llms.txt

TypeScript script for GitHub API integration that searches for duplicate issues and automatically closes them after a 3-day waiting period.

```typescript
#!/usr/bin/env bun

interface GitHubIssue {
  number: number;
  title: string;
  user: { id: number };
  created_at: string;
}

interface GitHubComment {
  id: number;
  body: string;
  created_at: string;
  user: { type: string; id: number };
}

async function githubRequest<T>(
  endpoint: string,
  token: string,
  method: string = 'GET',
  body?: any
): Promise<T> {
  const response = await fetch(`https://api.github.com${endpoint}`, {
    method,
    headers: {
      Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`,
      Accept: "application/vnd.github.v3+json",
      "User-Agent": "auto-close-duplicates-script",
      ...(body && { "Content-Type": "application/json" }),
    },
    ...(body && { body: JSON.stringify(body) }),
  });

  if (!response.ok) {
    throw new Error(
      `GitHub API failed: ${response.status} ${response.statusText}`
    );
  }

  return response.json();
}

// Script searches for duplicates and auto-closes after 3 days
```

### Execute Code Review Plugin Command

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/code-review/README.md

This snippet shows the basic command to initiate an automated code review on a pull request. Running this command in your terminal will trigger the plugin's analysis process.

```bash
/code-review
```

--------------------------------

### Automate Code Reviews with Claude Code's Code Review Plugin

Source: https://context7.com/anthropics/claude-code/llms.txt

This snippet demonstrates how to use the `/code-review` command from the Code Review plugin in Claude Code. The plugin automates PR reviews, providing confidence-based scoring to filter out false positives and focus on high-impact issues, streamlining the code review process.

```bash
/code-review
```

--------------------------------

### Utilize PR Review Toolkit Plugin in Claude Code

Source: https://context7.com/anthropics/claude-code/llms.txt

This snippet details the installation and usage of the `pr-review-toolkit` plugin, which provides specialized agents for comprehensive pull request analysis. It shows how to install the plugin and provides examples of natural language prompts to trigger specific review agents for tasks like test coverage evaluation, error handling, and type design.

```bash
/plugin install pr-review-toolkit
"Check if the tests cover all edge cases"
"Review the error handling in this PR"
"Is the documentation accurate?"
"Review type design for UserAccount"
"Before creating this PR, please:\n1. Review test coverage\n2. Check for silent failures\n3. Verify comments are accurate\n4. Review type design\n5. General code review"
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Custom Plugin Marketplaces for Claude Code

Source: https://context7.com/anthropics/claude-code/llms.txt

This JSON snippet demonstrates how to extend Claude Code's plugin system by adding extra known marketplaces. It allows users to specify custom repository URLs, enabling installation of plugins from private or organization-specific sources.

```json
{
  "extraKnownMarketplaces": [
    {
      "name": "company-plugins",
      "url": "https://github.com/your-org/claude-plugins"
    }
  ]
}
```

--------------------------------

### Initiate Feature Development Workflow with Claude Code Plugin

Source: https://context7.com/anthropics/claude-code/llms.txt

This snippet shows how to launch the `/feature-dev` plugin, which offers a 7-phase structured workflow for feature development. It leverages specialized agents like `code-explorer` and `code-architect` to ensure thorough analysis, proper architecture design, and quality assurance throughout the development cycle.

```bash
/feature-dev
```

--------------------------------

### Hooks System Configuration

Source: https://context7.com/anthropics/claude-code/llms.txt

JSON configuration defining pre/post hooks for tool execution including PreToolUse, PostToolUse, and SessionStart hooks with command-based validation and linting.

```json
{
  "hooks": {
    "PreToolUse": [
      {
        "matcher": "Bash",
        "hooks": [
          {
            "type": "command",
            "command": "python3 /path/to/validator.py"
          }
        ]
      }
    ],
    "PostToolUse": [
      {
        "matcher": "Edit",
        "hooks": [
          {
            "type": "command",
            "command": "npm run lint"
          }
        ]
      }
    ],
    "SessionStart": [
      {
        "type": "command",
        "command": "echo 'Session started'"
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Automate Git Operations with Claude Code

Source: https://context7.com/anthropics/claude-code/llms.txt

Claude Code can streamline Git workflows by generating commit messages and pull requests based on user prompts. It leverages standard Git commands and tools like `gh cli` to automate staging, committing, and creating PRs, ensuring consistent messaging and process adherence.

```bash
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Add user authentication feature

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
```

```bash
gh pr create --title "Add authentication" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- Implement JWT authentication
- Add login/logout endpoints
- Create auth middleware

## Test plan
- [ ] Test login flow
- [ ] Test token validation
- [ ] Test logout

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)
EOF
)"
```

--------------------------------

### Leverage Explore Subagent for Codebase Exploration

Source: https://context7.com/anthropics/claude-code/llms.txt

This snippet outlines the functionality of the Explore subagent in Claude Code, powered by Haiku 4.5, for fast codebase exploration. It automatically triggers for open-ended searches, efficiently searching code patterns to save context and answer questions about code structure and implementation, such as error handling.

```bash
# Fast codebase exploration powered by Haiku 4.5
# Automatically triggered for open-ended searches
# Saves context by efficiently searching code patterns
# Handles questions like "Where are errors handled?"

# Explore agent runs automatically when:
# - Searching for code patterns across large codebases
# - Understanding codebase structure
# - Finding implementations of specific features
```

### Select Claude Code AI Model

Source: https://context7.com/anthropics/claude-code/llms.txt

Claude Code allows users to interactively switch between different AI models or specify one at launch. This enables tailoring the AI's capabilities and cost-effectiveness for specific tasks, with options like Sonnet, Opus, and Haiku.

```bash
/model
```

```bash
claude --model claude-opus-4
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Claude Code Settings (settings.json)

Source: https://context7.com/anthropics/claude-code/llms.txt

This JSON configuration file provides extensive customization for Claude Code's behavior, covering tool permissions, sandbox settings, and external integrations like MCP servers. It allows developers to define fine-grained control over Claude's access and interactions within their environment.

```json
{
  "permissions": {
    "allowedTools": [
      "Read(**/*.{js,ts,json,md})",
      "Edit(**/*.{js,ts})",
      "Bash(git:*)",
      "Bash(npm:*)",
      "Bash(node:*)"
    ],
    "deniedTools": [
      "Edit(/config/secrets.json)",
      "Bash(rm -rf:*)"
    ]
  },
  "permissionMode": "acceptEdits",
  "spinnerTipsEnabled": true,
  "hooks": {
    "PreToolUse": [],
    "PostToolUse": []
  },
  "statusLine": {
    "enabled": true,
    "format": "{{model}} | {{tokens}}"
  },
  "sandbox": {
    "allowUnsandboxedCommands": false
  },
  "mcpServers": {
    "github": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@anthropic-ai/mcp-server-github"],
      "oauth": {
        "clientId": "your-client-id",
        "clientSecret": "your-client-secret",
        "scopes": ["repo", "issues"]
      }
    }
  },
  "extraKnownMarketplaces": [
    {
      "name": "company-plugins",
      "url": "https://github.com/your-org/claude-plugins"
    }
  ],
  "companyAnnouncements": {
    "enabled": true,
    "message": "Welcome! Check our internal docs..."
  }
}
```

### Integrate Code Review into Standard Pull Request Workflow

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/code-review/README.md

This snippet outlines the standard integration of the code review plugin into a typical pull request workflow. It shows the steps from creating a PR, running the automated review, to making fixes and merging the changes.

```bash
# Create PR with changes
/code-review

# Review the automated feedback
# Make any necessary fixes
# Merge when ready
```

--------------------------------

### Configure MCP Servers with JSON

Source: https://context7.com/anthropics/claude-code/llms.txt

This JSON configuration defines external Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers, such as GitHub and Filesystem, allowing Claude to interact with these services. It specifies the command to run the server, arguments, and optional OAuth credentials for authentication with services like GitHub.

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "github": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@anthropic-ai/mcp-server-github"],
      "oauth": {
        "clientId": "your-client-id",
        "clientSecret": "your-client-secret",
        "scopes": ["repo", "issues"]
      }
    },
    "filesystem": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem", "/path/to/allowed/files"]
    }
  }
}
```

```bash

/plugin enable security-guidance
/plugin disable security-guidance
/plugin marketplace
/plugin validate
```

# Automatically posts review comments
# Skip if review already exists

### Environment Configuration

Source: https://context7.com/anthropics/claude-code/llms.txt

Bash script setting up API keys and AWS configuration for Anthropic, OpenAI, and AWS Bedrock services with specific model defaults.

```bash
# API Keys
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...
export OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...

# AWS Bedrock
export AWS_REGION=us-west-2
export AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=...
export AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=...
# Default Sonnet model: global.anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929-v1:0
# awsAuthRefresh output displayed when auth is required
```

### Example Workflow for Code Review Plugin

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/code-review/README.md

This example demonstrates the typical usage and immediate outcomes of running the /code-review command on a PR branch. It illustrates how the plugin launches agents, scores issues, and posts comments based on a confidence threshold.

```bash
# On a PR branch, run:
/code-review

# Claude will:
# - Launch 4 review agents in parallel
# - Score each issue for confidence
# - Post comment with issues ≥80 confidence
# - Skip posting if no high-confidence issues found
```

### Example Automated Code Review Comment Format

Source: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/plugins/code-review/README.md

This markdown snippet illustrates the structured format of the review comments posted by the plugin. It includes a summary of issues found, detailed descriptions, and direct links to the relevant lines of code in the repository.

```markdown
## Code review

Found 3 issues:

1. Missing error handling for OAuth callback (CLAUDE.md says "Always handle OAuth errors")

https://github.com/owner/repo/blob/abc123.../src/auth.ts#L67-L72

2. Memory leak: OAuth state not cleaned up (bug due to missing cleanup in finally block)

https://github.com/owner/repo/blob/abc123.../src/auth.ts#L88-L95

3. Inconsistent naming pattern (src/conventions/CLAUDE.md says "Use camelCase for functions")

https://github.com/owner/repo/blob/abc123.../src/utils.ts#L23-L28
```

--------------------------------

### Manage Conversation Context in Claude Code

Source: https://context7.com/anthropics/claude-code/llms.txt

This section details commands for managing conversation context, including `/context` to check usage, `/add-dir` to include directories, and `/memory` for memory management. It also explains how `@-mentioning` directories automatically adds relevant files, and notes features like the Explore subagent and `/compact` command for context optimization.

```bash
# Check context usage
/context

# Add directory to context
/add-dir src/components

# Memory management
/memory

# Directory @-mentions:
# @-mention directories to include them in context
# Example: "Review the code in @src/components"
# Automatically adds relevant files to conversation context

# Context optimization:
# - Explore subagent reduces context usage
# - /compact command shrinks conversation size (respects existing boundaries)
# - Efficient file reading with selective context
# - Fixed "max_tokens must be greater than thinking.budget_tokens" error
```

--------------------------------

### Bash Command Validator Hook

Source: https://context7.com/anthropics/claude-code/llms.txt

Python script that validates bash commands using regex patterns, blocks dangerous commands, and returns appropriate exit codes to control tool execution flow.

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import json
import re
import sys

VALIDATION_RULES = [
    (r"^grep\b", "Use 'rg' instead of 'grep'"),
    (r"^rm -rf", "Dangerous command blocked")
]

def validate_command(command: str) -> list[str]:
    issues = []
    for pattern, message in VALIDATION_RULES:
        if re.search(pattern, command):
            issues.append(message)
    return issues

def main():
    input_data = json.load(sys.stdin)

    if input_data.get("tool_name") != "Bash":
        sys.exit(0)

    command = input_data.get("tool_input", {}).get("command", "")
    issues = validate_command(command)

    if issues:
        for message in issues:
            print(f"• {message}", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(2)  # Block tool call

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```
